Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Understanding the Go Runtime: The Scheduler 105 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Prefix sums at gigabytes per second with ARM NEON 35 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 72 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. The Met releases high-def 3D scans of 140 famous art objects 279 upvotes, 54 comments
  5. WolfIP: Lightweight TCP/IP stack with no dynamic memory allocations 127 upvotes, 21 comments
  6. Vite 8.0 Is Out 241 upvotes, 56 comments
  7. IMG_0416 (2024) 81 upvotes, 15 comments
  8. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 326 upvotes, 99 comments
  9. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 1244 upvotes, 448 comments
  10. Shall I implement it? No 1240 upvotes, 462 comments
  11. Celebrating Interesting Flickr Technologies 32 upvotes, 6 comments
  12. Willingness to look stupid 289 upvotes, 107 comments
  13. “This is not the computer for you” 397 upvotes, 159 comments
  14. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 303 upvotes, 119 comments
  15. Document poisoning in RAG systems: How attackers corrupt AI's sources 116 upvotes, 45 comments
  16. Innocent woman jailed after being misidentified using AI facial recognition 592 upvotes, 305 comments
  17. Launch HN: IonRouter (YC W26) – High-throughput, low-cost inference 62 upvotes, 25 comments
  18. Bringing Chrome to ARM64 Linux Devices 101 upvotes, 46 comments
  19. Show HN: Axe – A 12MB binary that replaces your AI framework 190 upvotes, 105 comments
  20. Bucketsquatting Is (Finally) Dead 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  21. Never Snooze a Future 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Worldwide Sidewalk Joy: Adding whimsy to neighborhoods 11 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Big data on the cheapest MacBook 350 upvotes, 278 comments
  24. Hyperlinks in Terminal Emulators 50 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. Are LLM merge rates not getting better? 146 upvotes, 133 comments
  26. ATMs didn’t kill bank teller jobs, but the iPhone did 406 upvotes, 435 comments
  27. Ceno, browse the web without internet access 3 upvotes, 0 comments
  28. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 352 upvotes, 407 comments
  29. Can you instruct a robot to make a PBJ sandwich? 26 upvotes, 28 comments
  30. Grief and the AI split 139 upvotes, 215 comments

Last generated 5:00pm, 13 March 2026. Source here.
